comments.author: @Channel22-z4s
comments.text: This place sounds super cool! I love learning about unique spots like St. Helena.

comments.author: @NovaTerrain-4K
comments.text: The documentary dives deep into the lives of the "Saints," their "Island Time" mindset, sustainable pole-and-line tuna fishing, and their world-famous, ultra-rare Green Tipped Bourbon coffee.

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: Saint Helena is a hidden gem with such a rich history and breathtaking landscapes. It is truly a unique corner of the world that I’d love to visit one day!

replies.author: @ImpossibleTravel38
replies.text: It really is a special place, hope you get to visit one day! ❤️❤️❤️

comments.author: @jimbutler2784
comments.text: Why are there so many fake pictures that are not St Helena at all?!

comments.author: @LMDiscoveryGlobe
comments.text: The concept of 'Island Time'—where people don't panic over delayed ships but just patiently adapt... the modern, fast-paced world desperately needs this mindset right now.

replies.author: @ImpossibleTravel38
replies.text: Glad you enjoyed that part of the documentary! ❤️❤️❤️

comments.author: @mohamedabdelkader8665
comments.text: Thank you for this meaningfulness has been in touch with us.Awesome work thank you for efforts.

replies.author: @ImpossibleTravel38
replies.text: Glad you liked it, have a wonderful day! ✈️✈️✈️

comments.author: @Aventurex-686
comments.text: Isolation can sometimes protect history better than walls ever could. St. Helena proves that being far away doesn't mean being forgotten. Meanwhile, I lose my TV remote almost every week inside the same house. What part of this island's story fascinated you most?

comments.author: @trueunrealglobe
comments.text: I honestly didn't expect to enjoy this documentary so much. Every location felt unique, and I ended up searching several of these places after finishing the video.

comments.author: @uncafunk
comments.text: Desert pronounced dessert? Must be AI voice-over

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: Would you actually live on St. Helena for a year if everything was free? Why or why not?

replies.author: @carlettewilliams
replies.text: No, too isolated…maybe I would visit.
